use serde::Serialize;

#[derive(Serialize)]
struct MetricsReport {
    cpu_usage: f64,
    memory_mb: u64,
    request_count: u64,
}

fn emit_batch_reports(reports: &[MetricsReport]) {
    for report in reports {
        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(report).unwrap();
        send_to_collector(&json);
    }
}

fn process_api_response(data: &serde_json::Value) -> String {
    serde_json::to_string_pretty(data).unwrap()
}

fn serialize_event(event: &Event) -> Vec<u8> {
    let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(event).unwrap();
    json.into_bytes()
}

fn send_to_collector(_s: &str) {}
struct Event;
impl serde::Serialize for Event {
    fn serialize<S: serde::Serializer>(&self, s: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
        s.serialize_unit()
    }
}

fn main() {
    let reports = vec![
        MetricsReport { cpu_usage: 0.5, memory_mb: 512, request_count: 1000 },
        MetricsReport { cpu_usage: 0.7, memory_mb: 768, request_count: 2000 },
    ];
    emit_batch_reports(&reports);
}
